to_company: SQM (Sociedad Quimica y Minera de Chile)
description: SQM's FY2025 20-F names Tianqi Lithium Corporation and its wholly owned subsidiary Inversiones TLC SpA as a principal shareholder owning approximately 21.9 percent of SQM, states that Tianqi is simultaneously a competitor in the lithium business and has the right to choose up to three SQM board members, and notes Chilean law limits SQM's ability to withhold competitively sensitive information from a director. The same filing discloses Tianqi is selling down: it has disposed of all its Series B shares and a board approved Series A disposal would reduce its stake to roughly 20.65 percent.

Albemarle's FY2023 10-K states that major competitors in lithium compounds include Sichuan Tianqi Lithium, among others, in the Business section describing the competitive landscape for lithium compounds.

Albemarle's FY2023 10-K states that major competitors in lithium compounds include Jiangxi Ganfeng Lithium, in the same sentence of the Business section describing the competitive landscape for lithium compounds that also names Tianqi.

Pilbara Minerals' ASX announcement dated 15 January 2024 discloses an amendment to its 2017 offtake agreement with Ganfeng Lithium Group, increasing spodumene concentrate supply from the Pilgangoora Operation from 160,000 tonnes per annum to up to 310,000 tonnes per annum over calendar years 2024 to 2026, priced at prevailing market rates.

Vale's FY2025 20-F states that at its Indonesian nickel operations, mineralized limonite ore from Sorowako and Pomalaa is sent to feed preparation plants and then to HPAL plants processed by joint ventures with Huayou Cobalt Co Ltd, described in the Properties section covering the PT Vale Indonesia operations.

A joint press release hosted on Glencore's own website, dated 29 May 2019, announces a long term revolving agreement for Glencore to supply cobalt hydroxide sourced from its KCC and Mutanda mining operations in the DRC to Umicore's battery materials value chain. ⚠ Evidence is the 2019 release itself, which predates every other source on this map by about six years, and it covers supply sourced partly from Mutanda. Whether the arrangement still holds has not been re-verified.

MP Materials' FY2022 Form 10-K states that outside of China, there are only two other producers operating at scale, MP Materials and Lynas Rare Earths Ltd, which processes its rare earth materials in Malaysia. ⚠ Evidence is a FY2022 filing, about three years older than the rest of this map's sources, and has not been re-checked against either company's latest filing.

Neo Performance Materials' 2025 Annual Information Form (for the year ended December 31, 2024) states that in the specialty chemicals markets, Neo competes primarily with two state owned enterprises in China as well as with Lynas Corporation Ltd.

USA Rare Earth's FY2025 Form 10-K states that internationally its primary competitors include Lynas Rare Earth Ltd, a leading non Chinese rare earth producer whose operations are largely focused on light rare earth elements with only limited heavy rare earth element production.

MP Materials' FY2024 10-K states that Shenghe Resources (Singapore) International Trading Pte. Ltd., an indirect majority owned subsidiary of Shenghe Resources Holding Co. Ltd., is contractually the exclusive distributor of MP's rare earth concentrate in China under the 2024 Offtake Agreement on a take or pay basis, and accounted for approximately 80% of MP's consolidated revenue in 2024.
Energy Fuels' FY2024 10-K states that to date, substantially all of the rare earth carbonate produced by its White Mesa Mill has been sold to Neo Performance Materials.

Energy Fuels' FY2024 10-K states that outside of China, Energy Fuels, Lynas (which has a primary monazite deposit at Mt. Weld in Western Australia) and the Saskatchewan Research Council are among the few groups processing monazite for rare earths.

Energy Fuels' FY2024 10-K Competition section, discussing the highly competitive heavy mineral sands market its newly acquired Base Resources assets operate in, names Iluka Resources among the key industry participants and largest HMS producers.

Energy Fuels' FY2024 10-K Competition section, discussing the highly competitive heavy mineral sands market its newly acquired Base Resources assets operate in, names Rio Tinto among the key industry participants and largest HMS producers.

Syrah Resources' 2025 Annual Report (financial year ended 31 December 2025) states in its Offtake Agreements section that, as announced to ASX on 23 December 2021 and 29 December 2021, Syrah entered into an offtake agreement with Tesla to supply 8kt per annum of active anode material from Vidalia. ⚠ THE SAME REPORT DISCLOSES THAT TESLA HAS ALLEGED SYRAH DEFAULTED on delivering conforming active anode material samples, with a cure deadline of 1 June 2026 after which Tesla may terminate the agreement. That deadline has passed and no later disclosure has been read, so whether this offtake survives is unknown.

Syrah Resources' 2025 Annual Report (financial year ended 31 December 2025) states that, as announced to ASX on 24 February 2025, Syrah entered into an offtake agreement with Lucid Group, Inc. to supply approximately 7kt of active anode material over a three year term from Vidalia.

Syrah Resources' 2024 Annual Report (financial year ended 31 December 2024) describes a multi-year natural graphite offtake with POSCO Future M for supply to South Korea, and states that Syrah executed a long term supply agreement with the leading ex-China anode company, POSCO Future M; the later 2025 Annual Report does not mention POSCO Future M, so currency is uncertain.
